description: Fix bugs, resource leaks, and missing guards discovered during a systematic audit of the MCP server package (platform/mcp-server/src/).

userStories.title: Fix domain_all + null domain matching everything in session permissions
userStories.description: As a platform maintainer, I want the domain_all session permission scope to only match a specific domain (or be rejected when domain is null) so that a single 'Allow Always' on a null-domain confirmation does not silently auto-approve all subsequent browser tool calls.
acceptanceCriteria: isSessionAllowed returns false for domain_all rules where rule.domain is null and the queried domain is a non-null string
handleConfirmationResponse either rejects domain_all scope when pending.domain is null, or falls back to tool_domain scope
Existing tests for allow_always with domain_all scope continue to pass for non-null domains
New test covers the null-domain + domain_all edge case
userStories.notes: The bug is in platform/mcp-server/src/state.ts:239-240 — the domain_all case returns true when rule.domain === null (matching everything). The rule is created in platform/mcp-server/src/extension-handlers.ts:530-537 — when scope is domain_all, rule.tool is set to null but rule.domain keeps pending.domain. If pending.domain is null (tools invoked without a tab context), the resulting rule has tool=null AND domain=null, which matches every tool on every domain. Fix: either reject domain_all scope when domain is null (fall back to tool_domain), or change isSessionAllowed so domain_all with null rule.domain only matches when the queried domain is also null. The safer approach is to fall back to tool_domain in handleConfirmationResponse when pending.domain is null and scope is domain_all. Tests are in platform/mcp-server/src/extension-handlers.test.ts and platform/mcp-server/src/state.test.ts.

userStories.title: Make reloadCore state updates atomic to prevent partial corruption
userStories.description: As a platform maintainer, I want reloadCore to update state atomically so that a throw midway through the assignment sequence does not leave the server in an inconsistent state with a new registry but stale tool config or missing browser tool cache.
acceptanceCriteria: reloadCore builds all new state values in local variables before assigning any of them to state
If any step throws, all state fields retain their previous values
rebuildCachedBrowserTools and pruneStaleState run before state is swapped
userStories.notes: In platform/mcp-server/src/reload.ts:190-218, state.registry is set at line 195, then state.toolConfig, browserToolPolicy, etc. are set on subsequent lines. If isCliSkipConfirmation() or rebuildCachedBrowserTools throws, registry is updated but other fields are stale. Fix: compute all new values into local variables first, then assign them all at once after rebuildCachedBrowserTools and pruneStaleState succeed. Something like: const newRegistry = registry; const newToolConfig = {...config.tools}; etc. Then after rebuildCachedBrowserTools succeeds (passing a temporary state-like object), assign all fields in a batch.

userStories.title: Fix file watcher to pick up resource and prompt changes from tools.json
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want the file watcher to pick up changes to resources and prompts in tools.json (not just tools) so that I don't need a full POST /reload when modifying resources or prompts in dev mode.
acceptanceCriteria: handleToolsJsonChange re-reads resources and prompts from tools.json (not just tools)
The updatedFields object includes resources and prompts when they are present in the manifest
New or existing test verifies that resource/prompt changes are picked up by the file watcher
userStories.notes: In platform/mcp-server/src/file-watcher.ts:298-310, handleToolsJsonChange calls parseToolsJson which only extracts the tools array. The updatedFields on line 310 is { tools }, so resources and prompts from tools.json are silently dropped — the spread { ...plugin, ...updatedFields } preserves old data. Fix: modify parseToolsJson (or the code in handleToolsJsonChange) to also extract resources and prompts from the parsed manifest and include them in updatedFields. The manifest format is defined in platform/shared/src/manifest.ts — look at parsePluginManifest for the canonical parsing. Tests are in platform/mcp-server/src/file-watcher.test.ts.

userStories.title: Clean up stale activeNetworkCaptures entries on tab close and sync
userStories.description: As a platform maintainer, I want stale entries in state.activeNetworkCaptures to be cleaned up when tabs are closed or during tab sync so that the Set does not grow unboundedly over the server lifetime.
acceptanceCriteria: When a tab is removed (tab.stateChanged to 'closed' or tab removed from tab.syncAll), its ID is deleted from state.activeNetworkCaptures
pruneStaleState (or equivalent) removes activeNetworkCaptures entries for tab IDs that no longer exist in state.tabMapping
New test verifies cleanup of stale capture entries
userStories.notes: state.activeNetworkCaptures (a Set<number>) is added to in platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/enable-network-capture.ts:52 and removed in disable-network-capture.ts:20. But if a tab is closed without calling disable, the entry remains forever. The best place to add cleanup is in pruneStaleState (platform/mcp-server/src/reload.ts — search for pruneStaleState) which already cleans up other stale state on reload, and in the tab state change handler in platform/mcp-server/src/extension-handlers.ts (handleTabStateChanged, handleTabSyncAll). When the tab mapping removes a tab ID, also delete it from activeNetworkCaptures.

userStories.title: Cap sessionPermissions array and clear stale rules on reload
userStories.description: As a platform maintainer, I want sessionPermissions to have a bounded size and to be pruned of rules referencing plugins or tools that no longer exist after a config reload, so that the permissions array does not grow unboundedly.
acceptanceCriteria: sessionPermissions has a maximum size cap (e.g., 500 entries) — oldest entries are dropped when the cap is reached
pruneStaleState removes session permission rules that reference tools or plugins no longer in the registry
New test verifies the size cap and stale rule pruning
userStories.notes: state.sessionPermissions (defined in platform/mcp-server/src/state.ts, pushed to in platform/mcp-server/src/extension-handlers.ts:543) is an unbounded array. Unlike auditLog (capped at 500 in state.ts), sessionPermissions has no cap. Add a cap constant (e.g., MAX_SESSION_PERMISSIONS = 500) and enforce it in the push path. Also add cleanup in pruneStaleState (platform/mcp-server/src/reload.ts) to filter out rules whose tool names no longer exist in the current registry.

userStories.title: Add stdout/stderr size cap in spawnAsync
userStories.description: As a platform maintainer, I want spawnAsync to cap the amount of stdout/stderr it collects so that a malicious or buggy npm command cannot exhaust server memory within the 60s timeout window.
acceptanceCriteria: spawnAsync stops accumulating stdout/stderr chunks after a size limit (e.g., 10 MB)
If the limit is reached, the child process is killed and an error is returned
New test verifies the size cap behavior
userStories.notes: In platform/mcp-server/src/plugin-management.ts:64-89, spawnAsync collects all stdout/stderr chunks without any size limit. The 60s timeout limits the time window but not the throughput. Add a MAX_OUTPUT_SIZE constant (e.g., 10 * 1024 * 1024), track total bytes in the data handlers, and if the limit is reached, call child.kill() and reject the promise with a descriptive error. Tests are in platform/mcp-server/src/plugin-management.test.ts.

userStories.title: Fix concurrent WebSocket upgrade race in server-node.ts
userStories.description: As a platform maintainer, I want WebSocket upgrades to use per-request state instead of shared mutable variables so that concurrent upgrades do not corrupt each other's context.
acceptanceCriteria: upgradeContext and pendingUpgradeHeaders are scoped per-request (e.g., stored in a Map keyed by the request/socket) instead of shared module-level variables
The 'headers' event handler retrieves the correct pending headers for the specific upgrade being processed
userStories.notes: In platform/mcp-server/src/server-node.ts:271 and 286, pendingUpgradeHeaders and upgradeContext are module-level mutable variables shared across all upgrades. If two WebSocket upgrades arrive concurrently (e.g., during hot reload when old and new extension connections overlap), the second upgrade can overwrite the first's context. Fix: use a WeakMap<WebSocket, Record<string, string>> or similar per-connection approach. The 'headers' event handler (line 272) receives the raw headers array — the ws library provides the websocket being upgraded in the 'headers' callback's second parameter (check ws docs). Move upgradeContext into a Map keyed by the socket (Duplex) from the upgrade event. This is a delicate area — the adapter.upgrade() function (line 289) must communicate back to the upgrade event handler, and the 'headers' event must find the right pending headers.

userStories.title: Add missing wss.on('error') and dev-proxy httpServer.on('error') handlers
userStories.description: As a platform maintainer, I want the WebSocket server and dev-proxy HTTP server to have error handlers so that an unhandled error event does not crash the process.
acceptanceCriteria: WebSocketServer in server-node.ts has an 'error' event handler that logs the error
httpServer in dev-proxy.ts has an 'error' event handler that provides a clear error message for port-in-use (EADDRINUSE)
userStories.notes: 1) platform/mcp-server/src/server-node.ts:258-262 — the WebSocketServer has noServer: true but no error handler. Add wss.on('error', (err) => log.error('WebSocket server error:', err)) after creation. 2) platform/mcp-server/src/dev-proxy.ts:232-236 — httpServer has no error handler. Add httpServer.on('error', (err) => { if (err.code === 'EADDRINUSE') console.error(`Port ${PROXY_PORT} is already in use`); else console.error('Dev proxy error:', err); process.exit(1); }).

userStories.title: Avoid mutating caller's args object in handlePluginToolCall
userStories.description: As a platform maintainer, I want handlePluginToolCall to extract tabId without mutating the caller's args object so that upstream code retains the original arguments for logging, auditing, or retries.
acceptanceCriteria: handlePluginToolCall creates a shallow copy of args (or uses destructuring) before deleting tabId
The original args object passed by the caller is not modified
userStories.notes: In platform/mcp-server/src/mcp-tool-dispatch.ts:322-323, the function does `const tabId = typeof args.tabId === 'number' ? args.tabId : undefined; delete args.tabId;` which mutates the caller's object. Fix: use destructuring: `const { tabId: rawTabId, ...pluginArgs } = args; const tabId = typeof rawTabId === 'number' ? rawTabId : undefined;` then use pluginArgs downstream instead of args. This avoids modifying the original reference. Tests are in platform/mcp-server/src/mcp-tool-dispatch.test.ts.

userStories.title: Remove redundant registry rebuild in onManifestChanged callback
userStories.description: As a platform maintainer, I want the onManifestChanged callback to avoid redundantly rebuilding the registry since the file watcher already rebuilt it, saving CPU on unnecessary Ajv recompilation.
acceptanceCriteria: The onManifestChanged callback in reload.ts no longer calls buildRegistry — it uses the already-updated state.registry
The callback still notifies MCP clients and performs extension sync
userStories.notes: In platform/mcp-server/src/reload.ts, the onManifestChanged callback (search for 'onManifestChanged') receives the plugin name after the file watcher has already rebuilt state.registry with the updated plugin data. The callback then calls buildRegistry(Array.from(state.registry.plugins.values()), ...) again, producing an identical registry and redundantly recompiling Ajv validators. Fix: remove the buildRegistry call from the callback and use state.registry directly (it's already been updated by the file watcher). The callback should still call rebuildCachedBrowserTools, notify MCP clients of tools/list changes, and send sync.full to the extension.

userStories.title: Fix export-har partial data loss when WebSocket frame fetch fails
userStories.description: As a platform maintainer, I want export-har to not clear the HTTP request buffer if the subsequent WebSocket frame fetch fails, so that users don't lose HTTP request data on a partial failure.
acceptanceCriteria: When clear: true is passed, HTTP request buffer is only cleared after both the request fetch and WebSocket frame fetch succeed
If the WebSocket frame fetch fails, HTTP request data is still available for retry
userStories.notes: In platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/export-har.ts:199-216, when clear: true, both browser.getNetworkRequests and browser.getWebSocketFrames are dispatched with clear: true independently. If the first succeeds (clearing HTTP requests) but the second fails, the HTTP request data is lost. Fix: first fetch both without clear, then if both succeed and clear was requested, dispatch separate clear commands (or dispatch the clears only after both fetches complete). Alternatively, fetch both with clear: false, collect results, then dispatch clear operations separately. Look at the existing dispatch pattern in the file.
